domain: Icelandic saga oral tradition (semi-professional saga-men transmit multi-generational narratives; named teller chains close sagas — 'Þorvaldr told this saga'; cross-saga consistency from teller-chain intersection)
specific_mechanism: RAG memory architecture that embeds per-memory teller-chain provenance metadata enabling chain-length/authority retrieval filtering and cross-entry teller-intersection consistency checks.
llm_application: SAGA-CHAIN: a retrieval-memory architecture where every fact in the external memory carries a TELLER-CHAIN metadata field — an ordered list of (agent_id, op_type, timestamp, authority_score) tuples documenting all prior agents that retrieved/summarised/transformed this fact. Retrieval supports: (a) chain-length thresholds (reject facts whose chain length > L_max as too-many-hops noisy), (b) authority-weighted scoring (sum authority over chain), (c) cross-fact consistency via teller-intersection graph (two retrieved facts SHARING ≥ K tellers are deemed consistent with confidence f(K)). Differs from Prompt Provenance PROV-O (prompt-level not per-fact retrieval-level), AEVS source-text grounding (single-source not chain), ProvSEEK chain-of-thought (forensic only) by combining (a) per-fact teller-chain metadata, (b) chain-length/authority filtering, (c) teller-intersection consistency graph.
novelty_claim: Prompt Provenance, AEVS, ProvSEEK already exist for LLM provenance tracking. SAGA-CHAIN's specific cross-entry teller-intersection graph is a small extension. Recombination of existing provenance + retrieval + audit components.
